surfaced_from: PWA chat request 2026-05-26 04:35-04:36 UTC — John asked why he cannot read OpenClaw/Hermes conversation text, then said to add it to the backlog
affects: A2A2H PWA / inter-hemisphere transparency and auditability
capability_needed: Visible inter-hemisphere coordination transcript or audit view in the PWA
details: John cannot directly read OpenClaw-to-Hermes delegation traffic because the current A2A2H PWA renders only final user-facing chat messages. OpenClaw can quote or summarize Hermes results, but backend delegation requests/responses and coordination events are not exposed as a readable human-facing thread. This is an oversight/auditability gap rather than a secrecy requirement. The PWA should provide a safe 'show agent coordination' or transcript/audit view that lets John inspect inter-hemisphere messages, task handoffs, response summaries, timestamps, routing decisions, and errors without exposing secrets, raw chain-of-thought, private tool traces, or sensitive credentials.
search_trail: Reviewed BACKLOG-001 — original A2A2H PWA scope includes chat and push, but not a readable inter-hemisphere coordination transcript.
Reviewed BACKLOG-004 — voice mode extends the human interface, but does not cover text visibility into OpenClaw/Hermes coordination.
Reviewed BACKLOG-014 — reliable background notifications are related delivery work, but do not solve coordination transcript visibility.
Searched BACKLOG.md and logs/backlog for coordination, inter-hemisphere, conversation, audit, show agent, visible, transparency, and A2A — no existing backlog item covered this exact capability.
Observed live PWA behavior 2026-05-26: OpenClaw delegated a greeting to Hermes and could quote Hermes' reply, but John could not read the underlying conversation as a visible chat thread.
proposed_resolution: Add a PWA coordination/audit panel backed by durable event storage. Capture A2A delegation request metadata, sanitized prompt/response text when safe, task IDs, routing owner/phase, timestamps, and errors. Render it behind an authenticated UI toggle such as 'show agent coordination'. Treat user-facing chat as canonical for John-facing conversation, and the coordination panel as an inspectable audit/debug layer. Upstream any generally useful OpenClaw/A2A transcript hooks if the implementation touches framework-level behavior; otherwise keep it as a CTO A2A2H PWA feature.
blast_radius: Touches the PWA backend, chat/event database schema or event writer, frontend rendering, and possibly Hermes sidecar/OpenClaw delegation logging. Main risk is overexposure: the implementation must redact secrets and avoid raw chain-of-thought/private tool traces. Done correctly, it improves observability without changing OpenClaw or Hermes core behavior.
rollback_plan: Hide or disable the coordination/audit panel and stop writing the optional coordination event type. Keep normal PWA chat and A2A delegation behavior unchanged. If a schema migration is added, include a reversible migration or leave unused audit rows harmlessly inert.
resolution_notes:
Code repair committed pending runtime verification: PWA-originated Hermes requests/responses now write sanitized a2a_request/a2a_response rows to chat.db for the existing PWA A2A/JSON transcript toggles. Obvious token/secret/password/auth/cookie/private-key shapes and common pasted credential patterns are redacted before audit storage. Regression and safe security gates pass.

Runtime verified 2026-05-26T21:00Z: live authenticated PWA -> Hermes message wrote sanitized a2a_request/a2a_response rows to chat.db and exposed them through /api/messages; regression and safe security gates passed. Audit rows are currently duplicated by wrapper/sidecar paths, noted for future UX polish but not blocking visibility.

Resolved 2026-05-26: A2A coordination audit transcript logging shipped in commit fea0599 and was runtime-verified by John's Hermes audit-ping at 2026-05-26T20:58Z. Live authenticated PWA-to-Hermes traffic wrote sanitized a2a_request/a2a_response rows and exposed them via /api/messages.

Reopened 2026-05-26: backend audit rows existed but the visible phone UI was insufficient. Visible PWA UI shipped in commit 476e366 with SHELL_CACHE cto-shell-v5; do not reclose until John confirms he can see the toggle and inline coordination rows on his phone.

2026-05-26T23:56Z OpenClaw pump: added a regression test that pins the visible PWA coordination UI contract without closing the item. The test verifies the header contains the Show agent coordination toggle, app.js renders a2a_* rows with capability/findings/Raw JSON details, style.css hides A2A rows by default until body.show-a2a is set, and service-worker.js uses cache cto-shell-v7 so John's client refreshes. Runtime/user-visible closure still waits on John confirming the toggle on his phone.

2026-05-27T02:18Z OpenClaw pump: runtime-verified the live authenticated PWA serves the visible Show agent coordination UI. Authenticated GET / contains id="toggle-a2a" and the Show agent coordination label; app.js contains the a2a_* rendering path with capability/findings and Raw JSON details; style.css hides A2A rows by default until body.show-a2a; /api/messages exposes live a2a_request and a2a_response audit rows to the authenticated client. PWA routing+voice tests and safe security gates passed. Artifact: logs/repairs/BACKLOG-016-runtime-visible-a2a-ui-verification-2026-05-27T0218Z.md. Item remains open pending John/device confirmation on his phone.
escalation_log: Priority raised to P0 on 2026-05-26 per John: 'the pwa is worthless as is. raise all pwa issues to p0.'
verification_commitment: Visible UI to be verified by John: PWA header must show Show agent coordination; default OFF hides a2a_request/a2a_response rows; ON renders coordination rows inline with distinct styling, capability, summarized findings/status/error text, and collapsible raw JSON.
